step_by_step_thinking:
Chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLL) involves mature B-cells because several studies mention treatments like ibrutinib, which inhibit B-Cell Receptor Signaling Pathways typically found in mature B-cells. Therefore, the tumor cells arise from mature B-cells.
\boxed{A} Based on the information given about ibrutinib and its effect on B-cell receptor signaling pathways, along with the fact that CLL affects mature B-cells, we can conclude that the tumor cells in CLL derive from mature B-cells.
